sections.heading: Use Cases
sections.body:
You can use Atlas Data Federation to:

- Copy Atlas cluster data into Parquet or CSV (Comma-Separated Values) files written to
  AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service) buckets or Azure Blob Storage.

- Query across multiple Atlas clusters and online archives to get a
  holistic view of your Atlas data.

- Materialize data from aggregations across Atlas clusters, AWS (Amazon Web Services)
  S3 (Simple Storage Service) buckets, and Azure Blob Storage.

- Read and import data from your AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service) buckets or Azure Blob Storage into an Atlas
  cluster.

sections.heading: Get Started
sections.body:
Learn how to create and connect to a sample federated database instance in Atlas.

To get started, see [onboarding experience](adf-getting-started).

sections.heading: Advanced Security Options
sections.body:
Explore Atlas Data Federation advanced security options, including private endpoints,
authentication methods, and advanced user configuration options.

See [Atlas Data Federation Advanced Security Options](adf-advanced-security-options)
for more information.

sections.heading: Define Data Stores
sections.body:
Configure and define data sources for your federated database instance. Support for data
sources include MongoDB Atlas clusters, AWS S3 buckets, Azure Blob
storage containers, Google Cloud storage buckets, HTTP URLs, and
Online Archives.

See [Storage Configuration](config-adf).

sections.heading: Administration
sections.body:
Discover features to manage your MongoDB Atlas federated database instance.

See [Administer Federated Database Instances](adf-administration).

sections.heading: MQL
sections.body:
Learn how to query federated data using MongoDB Query Language (MQL).

See [UI](query-fdi).

sections.heading: SQL
sections.body:
Leverage the SQL Interface to query federated data sources.

See [Connect With SQL](sql-interface).

sections.heading: Data Federation Tutorials
sections.body:
Browse advanced tutorials for implementing practical use cases
for Atlas Data Federation.

See [Getting Started tutorial](adf-tutorials).

sections.heading: Features
sections.body:
MongoDB Atlas Data Federation supports multiple data formats and
provides you with various operations to interact with Atlas Data Federation.

See [Supported Features](supported-unsupported).

sections.heading: Limitations
sections.body:
See [Atlas Data Federation Limitations](df-limitations) for information
on unsupported features.
